title: Portfolio Management Services (PMS)
description:
Portfolio Management Services (PMS) is a professional investment service where a registered portfolio manager manages an individual's investment portfolio on their behalf. Unlike mutual funds which pool investor money, PMS manages each investor's portfolio separately and individually.

All PMS providers must be registered with SEBI under the SEBI (Portfolio Managers) Regulations 2020. The minimum investment amount for a legitimate PMS is Rs 50 lakhs — this threshold was set by SEBI to ensure PMS is offered only to investors with sufficient financial sophistication and capacity.

Fraudsters often misuse the PMS label to target high net worth individuals and businesspeople, promising superior returns through exclusive investment strategies. They collect large sums — sometimes crores — before disappearing. Warning signs include minimum investments below Rs 50 lakhs, guaranteed return promises, and requests for funds to personal accounts.
key_facts: Minimum investment in legitimate PMS is Rs 50 lakhs
All PMS providers must be registered under SEBI (Portfolio Managers) Regulations 2020
PMS manager must maintain separate accounts for each client
PMS providers must provide monthly portfolio statements
No PMS can guarantee returns — all investments carry market risk
PMS fees must be disclosed upfront in a formal agreement
PMS providers must have minimum net worth of Rs 5 crore
Investors must sign a formal Portfolio Management Agreement
red_flags: Minimum investment offered below Rs 50 lakhs
No SEBI PMS registration number provided
Requests for direct transfer to personal or unofficial account
No formal Portfolio Management Agreement provided
Manager claims extraordinary track record without audited proof
Unsolicited calls or WhatsApp messages offering PMS services
Offshore address or unverifiable company registration
Manager cannot provide SEBI registration certificate
Fee structure not disclosed in writing upfront
Pressure to invest quickly without time to review documents
how_to_verify: Search the firm name or registration number
Verify registration is current and not expired
Check SEBI enforcement orders for any actions against the firm
Ask the manager for their SEBI registration certificate
Verify the firm's registered office address matches SEBI records
Call SEBI helpline 1800 266 7575 to confirm registration
real_case: SEBI has taken action against multiple unregistered entities offering PMS-like services to retail investors. In several cases, operators collected crores from businesspeople promising 30-40% annual returns through exclusive trading strategies. SEBI's Track B verification would have instantly flagged these entities as unregistered.

quiz.question: What is the minimum investment amount for a legitimate SEBI registered PMS?
quiz.explanation: SEBI mandates minimum investment of Rs 50 lakhs for all registered PMS providers.

quiz.question: Under which SEBI regulation must all PMS providers be registered?
quiz.explanation: All PMS providers must be registered under SEBI (Portfolio Managers) Regulations 2020.

quiz.question: A PMS provider offers minimum investment of Rs 10 lakhs. What does this mean?
options: They are offering a special introductory rate
They are an unregistered entity — SEBI mandates Rs 50 lakhs minimum
They are a small boutique firm with special exemption
They are operating under state government approval
quiz.explanation: Minimum below Rs 50 lakhs is a clear sign of an unregistered entity. Legitimate PMS cannot legally offer below this threshold.

quiz.question: Can a legitimate SEBI registered PMS guarantee returns?
options: Yes if the manager has more than 10 years experience
Yes for a fixed income portfolio only
No — no PMS can legally guarantee returns
Yes if backed by a bank guarantee
quiz.explanation: No SEBI registered PMS can legally guarantee returns. All market-linked investments carry risk.

quiz.question: What document must a PMS provider sign with every client?
quiz.explanation: A formal Portfolio Management Agreement is mandatory. Without it, the arrangement has no regulatory standing.

quiz.question: A PMS manager asks you to transfer Rs 75 lakhs to his personal savings account. What should you do?
options: Transfer the funds — it is more convenient
Ask for a post dated cheque as security
Refuse — funds must go to a registered client account, never personal accounts
Transfer half now and half later
quiz.explanation: Funds must always go to a properly designated client account. Transfer to personal accounts is a clear fraud signal.

quiz.question: How often must a legitimate PMS provider send portfolio statements?
quiz.explanation: SEBI regulations require PMS providers to send monthly portfolio statements to all clients.

quiz.question: What is the minimum net worth requirement for a SEBI registered PMS provider?
quiz.explanation: SEBI requires PMS providers to maintain minimum net worth of Rs 5 crore at all times.

quiz.question: You receive an unsolicited WhatsApp message from someone claiming to offer exclusive PMS services with 35% annual returns. What should you do?
options: Express interest and ask for more details
Immediately verify their SEBI registration and reject if unregistered
Invest a small amount to test their performance
Share the message with friends who might be interested
quiz.explanation: Never respond to unsolicited investment offers. Verify SEBI registration immediately and report suspected frauds.

quiz.question: Where do you verify if a PMS provider is registered with SEBI?
options: SEBI official registry at sebi.gov.in → Portfolio Managers
quiz.explanation: Always verify on SEBI's official Portfolio Managers registry. Never rely on the firm's own materials.
